title: Masoor Dal Soup

subs.name: moong dal (yellow split) — slightly milder in flavour, same cook time
kale or fenugreek leaves (methi) — add at the same stage
omit entirely and dry-temper on a non-stick pot for an oil-free version

steps.text: Rinse the masoor dal in two changes of water until the water runs mostly clear. Red lentils require no soaking — they cook fast on their own.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot on medium. Add cumin seeds and let them sputter for 15 seconds. Add onion, garlic and ginger and sauté for 4-5 minutes until the onion turns soft and lightly golden.
Add tomatoes, turmeric, red chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir well and cook for 3-4 minutes until the tomatoes break down and the masala smells fragrant.

Add the rinsed lentils and 750 ml water. Stir, bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer, half-cover the pot and cook for 15-18 minutes until the lentils are completely soft and have melted into the soup base. Stir occasionally and add water if the soup thickens too much — the final consistency should pour from a ladle in a thick stream.

Stir in the spinach (if using) and cook 1 minute until wilted. Remove from heat, squeeze in lemon juice, check salt and garnish with coriander. Serve hot in deep bowls with a slice of whole-grain bread or simply as a standalone protein soup.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.

substitution_notes: masoor-dal → moong dal (yellow split) — slightly milder in flavour, same cook time; spinach → kale or fenugreek leaves (methi) — add at the same stage; oil → omit entirely and dry-temper on a non-stick pot for an oil-free version
